title: Handshake AI / QGIS Workflow Training
one_liner: Screenshots, screen recordings, and rubric checks become examples for future scientific software agents.
input_data: QGIS projects; Vagon recordings; screenshots; task rubrics; output files
features_or_variables: tool clicks; layer type; CRS; raster/vector operation; error recovery; acceptance criteria
model_or_method: software-use agents; imitation learning; workflow trace classification; Codex file-aware replay
output: validated task demos; replayable GIS workflows; evaluation sets for agents
next_6_months: Tag videos and screenshots by task, tool, file, and pass/fail outcome.
next_12_months: Convert demos into action traces and build a small evaluation harness for QGIS tasks.
next_24_months: Train or evaluate agents that can replay routine geospatial workflows inside a virtual desktop.
bottleneck: Scientific software work is visual, multi-step, and judged by domain-specific output quality.
why_important: This creates the bridge between human expert workflow data and trustworthy AI agents for geospatial software.

title: REE Thesis Knowledge Graph
one_liner: AI-assisted Excel node/edge tables become Gephi visuals today and queryable graph intelligence tomorrow.
input_data: Excel node/edge tables; GraphML; PDFs; thesis slides; Adobe graph exports
features_or_variables: deposit; mineral; host rock; paragenetic stage; fluid source; reaction type; evidence source
model_or_method: Neo4j graph database; graph embeddings; LLM extraction; entity resolution; Graph Data Science
output: ranked relationships; graph-backed slides; feature hypotheses for REE accumulation
next_6_months: Clean the schema and mark which edges are observed, inferred, or conceptual.
next_12_months: Move the graph into Neo4j and add source/evidence weights for Cypher queries.
next_24_months: Use graph ML to rank relationships and propose field/lab validation targets.
bottleneck: Research synthesis gets trapped in prose, slides, and manual spreadsheets instead of becoming queryable structure.
why_important: REE supply chains matter for energy technologies, and graph structure can help experts find patterns faster.

title: Processing Earthquake Globe
one_liner: The first AI-assisted geoscience visualization used USGS earthquake data, 3D spatial encoding, and sound.
input_data: USGS earthquake events; latitude/longitude; depth; magnitude; event time
features_or_variables: location; depth; magnitude; time window; audio frequency/intensity mapping
model_or_method: interactive 3D visualization; clustering; anomaly tagging; sonification features
output: web-rebuilt seismic globe with filters, provenance, and explainable narration
next_6_months: Recover or export the verified 54-second LinkedIn video and rebuild the data schema.
next_12_months: Prototype a Three.js or Streamlit globe with reproducible USGS event pulls.
next_24_months: Add ML-assisted event clustering and viewer-facing explanations of seismic patterns.
bottleneck: Early creative visualizations often disappear because code, data, and captures are not preserved together.
why_important: It shows how geoscience data can become more intuitive when visual and auditory signals are designed together.

title: Pondicherry Seismic Notebooks
one_liner: Exploratory earthquake notebooks become a reproducible waveform, picking, and uncertainty workflow.
input_data: waveforms; station metadata; earthquake catalog events; notebook outputs
features_or_variables: arrival time; distance; SNR; magnitude; station geometry; event location
model_or_method: arrival-picking ML; QA classifier; uncertainty estimation; reproducible ObsPy pipeline
output: velocity tables; annotated waveforms; maps; validation report
next_6_months: Standardize notebook outputs and preserve event/station provenance.
next_12_months: Add assisted arrival picks with human review and QA flags.
next_24_months: Build a repeatable seismic interpretation dashboard with uncertainty summaries.
bottleneck: Small seismic studies get stuck between exploratory notebooks and defensible reproducible analysis.
why_important: Better waveform QA helps turn under-monitored regions into clearer geologic and water-resource interpretations.

title: North Slope Gas Hydrate Atlas
one_liner: Public geologic sources, maps, and well-log concepts become a hydrate screening pipeline.
input_data: well logs; geologic maps; stratigraphy; source library; depth/temperature context
features_or_variables: gamma ray; resistivity; density; porosity; formation; overburden; pressure-temperature proxies
model_or_method: feature engineering; explainable hydrate classifier; provenance audit; uncertainty ranking
output: ranked hydrate intervals; source-backed atlas; expert review queue
next_6_months: Define a trusted feature table and attach provenance to every row.
next_12_months: Build screening models and compare outputs to known hydrate/geologic context.
next_24_months: Create an expert-in-the-loop dashboard for hydrate targets and uncertainty.
bottleneck: Subsurface data is fragmented across maps, papers, logs, and inconsistent assumptions.
why_important: Hydrate screening connects directly to energy planning, methane risk, and future resource evaluation.

title: Rock Classification And Geochemistry
one_liner: Thin-section, chemical, and graph evidence can become multimodal labeled training data.
input_data: thin-section images; chemical diagrams; spider plots; formation CSVs; class decks
features_or_variables: mineral class; texture; chemistry; REE pattern; host rock; alteration features
model_or_method: multimodal classifier; image-text-tabular embedding; expert label audit
output: ranked rock/mineral labels; training examples; model error review
next_6_months: Inventory images and diagrams with labels and source context.
next_12_months: Build a small supervised dataset and baseline classifier.
next_24_months: Test multimodal models that explain which visual/geochemical cues drove a label.
bottleneck: Rock labels, petrographic context, and geochemical plots are rarely stored as one training example.
why_important: Classification support can speed teaching, field/lab review, and data preparation without replacing expert judgment.

title: SAGE / Valles Caldera Geophysics
one_liner: Field geophysics maps become a fusion and interpretation dashboard for expert review.
input_data: gravity; EM/TEM/ERT; seismic; field notes; geologic maps; presentation figures
features_or_variables: anomaly; conductivity; velocity; density; formation; terrain constraint; uncertainty
model_or_method: data fusion; anomaly segmentation; map comparison assistant; uncertainty tagging
output: interpretation dashboard; figure provenance; field-review queue
next_6_months: Attach each map to method, source, and uncertainty notes.
next_12_months: Create side-by-side comparison views for gravity, EM, seismic, and geology.
next_24_months: Use ML-assisted anomaly segmentation while keeping expert review visible.
bottleneck: Field geophysics requires comparing imperfect methods without flattening uncertainty.
why_important: Energy and water projects need interpretations that preserve uncertainty across instruments and terrain.

title: Supervised ML For Moho Depth Mapping
one_liner: Gravity/Moho training data becomes a test case for honest transfer learning across regions.
input_data: Australian Moho data; gravity grids; U.S. Moho targets; notebook outputs; model report
features_or_variables: gravity anomaly; location; crustal proxy; Moho depth; region split; residual error
model_or_method: ANN baseline; ensemble comparison; spatial cross-validation; transfer testing; residual mapping
output: predicted Moho depth; error maps; transfer-score discussion; model-risk checklist
next_6_months: Recover the notebook/report and document exact variables, split logic, and model score.
next_12_months: Compare ANN, tree ensembles, and spatial validation with leakage checks.
next_24_months: Build a reproducible ML demo showing where the model transfers and where it fails.
bottleneck: Geoscience ML can look good on familiar data but fail when moved to a new basin or continent.
why_important: Honest transfer testing is critical before ML is trusted for energy or subsurface decisions.

title: AI-Assisted App Building And Data Pipelines
one_liner: Local notebooks and downloads become a GitHub-tracked app workflow with model-risk discussion.
input_data: local downloads; notebook charts; Streamlit app files; GitHub commits; pipeline outputs
features_or_variables: ticker features; freshness; train/test window; leakage flag; model score; chart output
model_or_method: Codex app scaffolding; pipeline automation; baseline models; cloud/virtual desktop workflow
output: tracked app; refreshed charts; model-risk notes; discussion prompts
next_6_months: Separate training and unseen evaluation windows and document leakage risks.
next_12_months: Move more of the workflow into GitHub/cloud refreshes and lightweight dashboards.
next_24_months: Use AI agents to maintain the pipeline while preserving human review of model claims.
bottleneck: Young builders can ship apps quickly now, but model confidence can be fake without careful evaluation.
why_important: AI-assisted app building can create opportunity while teaching better habits around evidence and risk.
